orchestrator_notes: RT-1 / Enhancement #36. fetch_context() forces agents to make 5 sequential MCP calls (one per category) instead of 1 batch call. The MCP schema already accepts a categories ARRAY — investigate whether the backend actually processes multiple categories or silently takes only the first. Every orchestrator hits this. P2 severity, E1 effort.

sessions.title: Research: fetch_context() Single-Category-Per-Call Limitation
planned_tasks: Read the older context.py:173-251 fetch_context (check if this is dead code or a different path)
Read ToolAccessor.fetch_context (tool_accessor.py:594-637) to understand the pass-through
Trace the FULL runtime flow: MCP tool call -> mcp_http.py dispatch -> tool_accessor -> fetch_context function
Determine: does the function iterate over the categories array or only process one?
Check if categories=['vision_documents','tech_stack','architecture'] actually returns all 3 or just the first
Read the MCP schema definition for fetch_context in mcp_http.py (~line 669) — does it document single vs batch?
Read thin_prompt_generator.py and protocol_builder.py — how are agents instructed to call fetch_context?
Check if there's a token budget or response size constraint that makes batching impractical
If single-category is by design, document WHY (token limits? response size? sequential dependency?)
If it's a bug/oversight, propose the fix to enable true batch category fetching
tasks_completed: Traced full runtime flow: mcp_http.py -> tool_map -> ToolAccessor.fetch_context -> context_tools/fetch_context.py:fetch_context
Confirmed ToolAccessor.fetch_context (tool_accessor.py:626) imports from context_tools/fetch_context.py (active impl)
Confirmed context.py:fetch_context (lines 173-251) is NOT dead code — separate entry point for agent executors (takes agent_id, resolves product_id, delegates to same context_tools/fetch_context.py)
Read full fetch_context() body — THREE explicit guards reject multi-category (Handover 0351)
Read _fetch_category() — dispatches to per-category internal tools via CATEGORY_TOOLS dict
Read _flatten_results() — exists but NEVER called in current code (dead code due to single-category enforcement)
Read MCP schema (mcp_http.py:669-700) — MISLEADING: advertises default=['all'] and includes 'all' in enum, but backend rejects both
Read thin_prompt_generator.py — orchestrator told to call get_orchestrator_instructions() which returns per-category framing pointers
Read protocol_builder.py STEP 2 — get_orchestrator_instructions() returns context_fetch_instructions with critical/important/reference tiers
Read MissionPlanner._build_fetch_instructions() — builds individual per-category fetch instructions based on user priority config
Confirmed token budgets: categories range from ~100 (product_core) to 24K (vision_documents full) — batching all could yield ~42K tokens
Documented design rationale and proposed fixes

dead_code_paths.status: NOT dead code — separate entry point for agent executors. Takes agent_id + tenant_key, resolves product_id/project_id via AgentExecution/AgentJob/Project/Product join, then delegates to the SAME context_tools/fetch_context.py function. Used by executor-facing context endpoint.
DEAD CODE — exists but never called. Was designed for output_format='flat' with multi-category responses, but single-category enforcement makes it unreachable.

findings.categories_handling: THREE explicit guards enforce single-category (Handover 0351): (1) categories is None -> error SINGLE_CATEGORY_REQUIRED, (2) 'all' in categories -> error ALL_NOT_ALLOWED, (3) len(categories) > 1 -> error SINGLE_CATEGORY_REQUIRED. After guards: category = categories[0], processes exactly one category via _fetch_category() dispatch.

response_structure.multi_category_response: N/A — rejected with error before processing

agent_instructions_say.mcp_schema: MISLEADING — mcp_http.py:681-700 defines categories as array with enum including 'all', default=['all'], description says 'Categories to fetch. ["all"] for everything.' But backend rejects both 'all' and multi-category.
agent_instructions_say.thin_prompt_orchestrator: Orchestrator told to call get_orchestrator_instructions() (Step 2 in protocol). This returns context_fetch_instructions with per-category framing pointers organized by priority tier (critical/important/reference). Each pointer is a single-category fetch_context call.
agent_instructions_say.protocol_builder: STEP 2 references get_orchestrator_instructions() which returns framing instructions. The framing architecture (MissionPlanner._build_fetch_instructions) generates individual per-category instructions based on user priority config.
agent_instructions_say.explanation: Agents are not EXPLICITLY told 'one category at a time', but the framing architecture structures each category as an individual fetch instruction, so orchestrators naturally call one at a time.

design_reasons: Token budget safety — categories range from ~100 to 24K tokens each; batching all could produce ~42K tokens in a single MCP response, risking context window overflow
SaaS security — Code-level enforcement that LLMs cannot bypass via prompt injection (comment: 'Code-level enforcement - LLM cannot bypass via prompt injection')

Priority-based fetching — framing architecture enables orchestrators to fetch CRITICAL categories first (product_core, memory_360), then IMPORTANT (tech_stack, architecture), then REFERENCE (testing, git_history). Sequential calls allow priority ordering.

Depth control per category — each category has independent depth settings (vision_documents: light/medium/full, memory_360: last_n_projects, git_history: commits). Single-category calls allow precise depth control.
Introduced in Handover 0351 as an intentional security/reliability constraint

token_budget_details.worst_case_batch_all: ~42K tokens — would overwhelm most agent context windows

mcp_schema_inconsistency.issue: MCP schema (mcp_http.py:681-700) advertises categories=['all'] as default and includes 'all' in enum, but backend rejects it. This misleads agents and wastes a failed call if they try the default.
mcp_schema_inconsistency.schema_says: default: ['all'], enum includes 'all', description: 'Categories to fetch. ["all"] for everything.'
mcp_schema_inconsistency.backend_does: Returns error ALL_NOT_ALLOWED with message 'categories=["all"] is not allowed'
mcp_schema_inconsistency.severity: Low — agents learn from error response, but wastes one round-trip

proposed_fixes.name: Approach A: Fix Misleading MCP Schema
proposed_fixes.description: Remove 'all' from categories enum, remove default=['all'], update description to explicitly state 'Exactly ONE category per call. Call separately for each category.' This prevents agents from trying invalid batch calls.
proposed_fixes.rationale: Zero-risk fix. Eliminates the misleading schema that contradicts the backend enforcement. Agents will no longer waste a call trying categories=['all']. No backend changes needed.

proposed_fixes.name: Approach B: Close as By-Design
proposed_fixes.description: Document the single-category-per-call constraint as intentional (Handover 0351). Update Enhancement #36 to closed-wontfix. The framing architecture is the designed pattern for orchestrator context fetching.
proposed_fixes.rationale: Valid but leaves the misleading MCP schema in place. Agents will still waste calls trying categories=['all'].

proposed_fixes.name: Approach C: Schema Fix + Agent Parallelism Hint
proposed_fixes.description: Fix MCP schema (Approach A) AND add a hint in the framing instructions that agents CAN make multiple fetch_context calls in parallel (as separate MCP tool calls, each with one category). This is already possible but agents may not realize it.
proposed_fixes.rationale: Best of both worlds: fixes misleading schema AND encourages agents to parallelize their single-category calls. 5 parallel calls is nearly as fast as 1 batch call, while maintaining per-category token budget safety.

files_investigated: src/giljo_mcp/tools/context.py (fetch_context — alternate entry point for agent executors)
api/endpoints/mcp_http.py (MCP schema definition, lines 265-274 and 669-714; tool_map dispatch, line 880)
src/giljo_mcp/thin_prompt_generator.py (orchestrator startup prompt, continuation prompt, 360 memory hint)
sessions.notes_for_next: 0768b should focus on Approach A+C: (1) Fix the misleading MCP schema by removing 'all' from enum and updating description. (2) Add parallelism hint to framing instructions so agents call 5 categories in parallel rather than sequentially. The single-category-per-call constraint is correct and should be maintained. Do NOT implement batch support — the token budget safety is intentional. The _flatten_results function is dead code and could be removed as cleanup.
sessions.summary: fetch_context() single-category-per-call is BY DESIGN (Handover 0351). Three explicit code guards reject multi-category and 'all' requests. Design serves token budget safety (~42K worst case if batched), SaaS security (code-level LLM enforcement), and priority-based fetching. The MCP schema is MISLEADING — advertises default=['all'] but backend rejects it. Recommended fix: update MCP schema to match reality + add parallelism hint so agents can fire 5 single-category calls simultaneously.

sessions.title: Implement: Fix Misleading fetch_context Schema + Dead Code Removal
planned_tasks: Fix misleading MCP schema: remove 'all' from enum, remove default=['all'], update description
Optionally add parallelism hint to mission_planner framing instructions
tasks_completed: Read 0768a findings — confirmed Approach A+C (schema fix + parallelism hint)

Fixed MCP schema: removed 'all' from categories enum, removed default=['all'], updated description to 'Exactly ONE category per call. To fetch multiple categories, make parallel tool calls — one per category.'
Verified _flatten_results has zero callers (grep across entire codebase — only in handovers and chain logs)
Skipped Fix 4 (parallelism hint in mission_planner) — _build_fetch_instructions returns structured data not text, and the schema description already includes the parallelism hint
Ran full test suite — 1390 passed, 0 failed
deviations: Combined with 0767b implementation (json.dumps safety net) per orchestrator handover 0767b+0768b
Skipped Fix 4 (parallelism hint in mission_planner) — the method returns data structures, not text instructions. The parallelism guidance is already in the updated schema description.
sessions.summary: Fixed misleading fetch_context MCP schema that advertised default=['all'] and included 'all' in enum despite the backend rejecting both. Agents no longer waste a failed call trying invalid defaults. Removed dead _flatten_results() function (unreachable since Handover 0351 single-category enforcement). Combined commit with 0767b fix.

chain_summary: Chain complete. 0768a confirmed single-category-per-call is by design (token budget safety, SaaS security). 0768b fixed the misleading MCP schema and removed dead code. No batch support added — the existing architecture with parallel single-category calls is the correct pattern.
